bullets.summary: Nvidia reported Q4 fiscal-2025 results on 2026-02-26. Revenue guidance for fiscal Q1 2026 came in at $43 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $42.3 billion, with management citing Blackwell GPU demand that 'continues to exceed expectations.' The forward visibility figure cited — half a trillion dollars in Blackwell and Vera Rubin revenue from start of 2026 through year-end — was the headline number driving the post-earnings move.

A separate Yahoo Finance piece framed the $65 billion revenue forecast (a figure attributed to a later fiscal quarter) as confirmation the AI infrastructure build-out is intact; the article noted an operating margin of 63.2% and flagged trade-control risk on H20 chips to China as the primary watch item going forward.

Alphabet Q4 2025 earnings (reported early February 2026): Google Cloud revenue rose 48% year-over-year to $17.7 billion, well above the $16.2 billion consensus. Management announced 2026 capex guidance of $175–$185 billion, roughly doubling 2025 spend, explicitly tied to AI infrastructure. Alphabet stock fell on the capex sticker shock before recovering.

Yahoo Finance highlighted that Gemini 3 model benchmarks outperformed competing models and that Alphabet secured a landmark AI deal with Apple during the period, reinforcing GOOGL's dual role as an AI platform and quantum research leader. Note: GOOGL carries a secondary quantum bucket exposure via Google Quantum AI's Willow chip program, though no new quantum milestone was announced in the Feb 2026 window specifically.

Microsoft Q2 fiscal-2026 earnings (reported 2026-01-29, within the 30-day lookback): Non-GAAP EPS of $4.14 beat estimates by 6.7%; Azure and other cloud services revenue surged 39% year-over-year, well above expectations. Commercial bookings jumped 230%, reflecting large Azure commits. The Intelligent Cloud segment ($32.9 billion, 40% of total revenue) is now the company's largest segment by revenue share.

Microsoft reported 150 million monthly active Copilot users and 900 million MAUs of AI features as of the Q2 call — the most concrete AI monetization disclosure from any of the hyperscalers to date, suggesting that the AI wave has moved from infrastructure capex into recurring revenue at scale.

Rocket Lab won an $816 million contract from the U.S. Space Development Agency in early February 2026 to design and manufacture 18 missile-defense satellites, with deliveries running through 2029. This is the company's largest-ever single award and marks a strategic shift from launch-only to integrated space systems. The stock initially surged ~22% on the announcement before giving back roughly half the gain.

Rocket Lab also secured a new multi-launch Electron contract with BlackSky (4 dedicated missions), bringing total BlackSky launches to 17 since 2019, and completed the acquisition of Optical Support, Inc. to strengthen national-security payload capability — all within February 2026, illustrating the company's rapid vertical integration.

The Robot Report's January 2026 state-of-industry report (the most current curated-source piece in the lookback window) noted that humanoid and collaborative robot deployments are accelerating, led by Tesla Optimus (targeting 1 million units/year) and Agility Robotics' Digit in logistics. BOTZ has ~42% industrial-robot exposure (Fanuc, ABB, Yaskawa, Mitsubishi Electric) and benefits from both humanoid hype and traditional industrial automation revenue.

An IEEE survey published in early 2026 found that AI-driven embodied robotics is the #1 expected growth area for the year, with China outpacing the U.S. in humanoid iteration speed due to a deeper hardware supply chain. BOTZ's heavy Japan and European industrial-robot weighting means it captures the traditional revenue side but has limited direct exposure to the China humanoid momentum.

ARK Invest entered 2026 with aggressive gene-editing accumulation: more than 195,000 shares of Beam Therapeutics added across ARKK and ARKG, plus incremental buys in CRISPR Therapeutics, Pacific Biosciences, Twist Bioscience, and Personalis. CRISPR Therapeutics remains the largest ARKG holding at ~9.4% of assets. The pattern signals Cathie Wood is front-running clinical-readout catalysts in precision-medicine names.

A separate Yahoo Finance piece reported that new gene-editing trial results provided a positive bump to ARKG during February, consistent with the fund's high-beta, catalyst-driven profile. No specific FDA action or major regulatory event was flagged in the curated sources for the 30-day window.

Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) crossed $4 billion in AUM and earned a 5-star Morningstar rating, reflecting sustained investor inflows into quantum-computing exposure. The ETF was trading at approximately $117–$116 during the last week of February 2026, essentially flat on the month. No new hardware milestone from QTUM's underlying holdings was announced specifically in February 2026.

Quantinuum's anticipated IPO was flagged by analysts as the biggest pending catalyst that could reshape QTUM's composition and AUM materially in 2026, but no IPO date was set as of end-February. The Quantum Computing Report noted an IQM/Riverlane/Zurich Instruments real-time error-correction platform development in the window — a research-cadence milestone relevant to QTUM underlying names, not a revenue event.

The Trump Media / TAE Technologies $6 billion merger, announced December 2025, remained the dominant NUKZ-adjacent headline during February. On 2026-02-27, Trump Media opened separate discussions about spinning off Truth Social, adding deal-structure uncertainty. The TAE merger is the highest-profile fusion transaction in the ETF's investable universe and is expected to close mid-2026.

NUKZ surged roughly 58% over the trailing year heading into February 2026, driven by Microsoft and Amazon data-center nuclear-power announcements and concentrated positions in Cameco (>10% of the fund). The fund's $725 million AUM and exposure to uranium miners, reactor manufacturers, and utilities spans both fission and fusion themes, but the near-term revenue base is overwhelmingly fission/uranium.

The Federal Reserve held its target rate at 3.50–3.75% (unchanged since December 2025) through the end of February 2026, providing a stable but unexciting yield backdrop for AGG. With the Fed on pause, AGG's performance is driven primarily by duration risk: any uptick in long-end yields compresses NAV while the short-end income is capped.

On 2026-02-28, the U.S. and Israel launched airstrikes on Iran, sending commodity prices sharply higher and import prices up 1.3% — the largest single-month jump since March 2022. This late-February geopolitical shock creates a re-inflation risk that weighs on AGG: higher inflation expectations push yields up and bond prices down.

BIL (SPDR Bloomberg 1–3 Month T-Bill ETF) tracks 1–3 month Treasury bills. With the Fed funds rate at 3.50–3.75% and unchanged through end-February, BIL's annualized yield remained near that band — roughly 3.5% gross. No curated source surfaced BIL-specific news for the window; fell back to open search which returned only general performance pages.

The late-February Iran airstrike risk introduced mild safe-haven demand for short-dated Treasuries, but with the Fed on hold the primary BIL risk is opportunity cost: if the Fed eventually cuts, BIL's yield compresses faster than longer-duration bonds reprice upward.

IAU (iShares Gold Trust) closed at approximately $94.75 on 2026-02-13, up ~2.5% on the day, and returned nearly 49% over the trailing twelve months — the strongest trailing-year return among traditional asset classes. Central bank gold buying and USD weakness were cited as the structural drivers.
The 2026-02-28 Iran conflict news provided an additional late-month demand catalyst for gold as a geopolitical hedge, reinforcing IAU's role as the portfolio's defensive hard-asset position.

VIG (Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF) had a muted February: the fund experienced extra volatility relative to the broader market, with tech-heavy peers outperforming due to AI earnings momentum. The dividend machinery of VIG's holdings remained intact — the most recent quarterly dividend was $0.83/share (March 2026 ex-date), consistent with its track record of growing distributions.

A late-February note from Yahoo Finance described early-2026 VIG underperformance vs. growth names as a function of the AI earnings surge rather than any VIG-specific problem, suggesting the gap could narrow in a risk-off rotation — the Iran airstrike on Feb 28 being exactly the kind of event that could trigger such a rotation.

AI.rationale: All three AI-bucket tickers reported revenue acceleration with real, growing cash flows in the lookback window: NVDA's half-trillion-dollar demand visibility and 63% operating margins, MSFT's 39% Azure growth with 150 million Copilot MAUs, and GOOGL's 48% Cloud growth with $175–185 billion capex commitment. Revenue is compounding, enterprise adoption is measurable, and supply is still constrained — classic surge profile. Not peak yet because hyperscaler capex guides continued heavy investment rather than signs of excess inventory or buyer fatigue.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: RKLB won its largest-ever contract ($816 million, defense satellites), added a commercial multi-launch deal, and completed an acquisition — all in one month. Government and commercial revenue is materializing, and the company is integrating vertically. The sector is past quiet buildup and into real contract flow, but is not yet at peak enthusiasm given the stock gave back half its post-announcement gain.

robotics.rationale: The Robot Report's state-of-industry data confirms rapid technology development (humanoid, collaborative, embodied AI) but commercial deployment at scale is still nascent. BOTZ's underlying industrial-robot holdings (Fanuc, ABB, Yaskawa) generate real revenue, but the humanoid/embodied AI sub-theme — which is driving current enthusiasm — has not yet produced meaningful recurring revenue. The wave is advancing fast but is still in its early adoption ramp.

engineered_biology.rationale: ARKG's ARK is actively front-loading gene-editing bets (Beam Therapeutics, CRISPR Therapeutics) ahead of clinical catalysts, and a positive trial readout bumped the fund in February — but these are pre-revenue or early-revenue events. No blockbuster FDA approval or commercial launch was surfaced in the window. The wave is alive and attracting capital, but remains squarely in buildup.

quantum.rationale: QTUM crossed $4 billion AUM and earned a 5-star rating, showing investor appetite, but the Quantinuum IPO (the wave's biggest pending commercial event) had no date set, and the only technical milestone in the window (IQM error-correction platform) is a research development rather than a revenue event. The wave is early and pre-commercial — firmly buildup.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: NUKZ's 58% trailing-year gain partly reflects genuine enthusiasm, but the fund's near-term revenue base is uranium miners and fission utilities, not fusion. The TAE Technologies merger (the most visible fusion deal) is a speculative transaction still awaiting closure. The fusion sub-theme has no commercial revenue yet; the fission sub-theme is in early renaissance. Overall: buildup, with the fission component slightly further along than the fusion component.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU, and VIG are macro instruments without a technology-wave attachment. The relevant backdrop (Fed on hold at 3.50–3.75%, IAU at multi-year highs, VIG lagging growth) describes a regime, not a wave cycle stage.

thematic_concerns.concern: The fund markets itself under a 'nuclear fusion' banner but its actual revenue base is dominated by uranium miners and fission utilities (Cameco >10% of AUM). Fusion itself is pre-commercial. An investor expecting fusion exposure should verify the fund's actual holdings composition against the profile's 'nuclear fusion' wave intent.
thematic_concerns.evidence: NUKZ ETF Surges 58% article notes Cameco >10% of the fund with top 3 holdings at 21% of assets, all from the fission/uranium sector. TAE Technologies merger (the fund's primary fusion link) is not yet a fund holding — it is a speculative SPAC-style transaction expected to close mid-2026.

thematic_concerns.concern: Late-February geopolitical shock (Iran airstrikes, 2026-02-28) drove a 1.3% monthly jump in import prices, the largest since March 2022. If this becomes a persistent re-inflation signal, the Fed-on-hold backdrop could shift to rate-hike risk, which would pressure AGG's NAV via duration losses — directly in tension with the profile's desire to hold bonds as a portfolio stabilizer.
thematic_concerns.evidence: Yahoo Finance AGG history page noted the Iran conflict event and its commodity/import-price impact on 2026-02-28.
